Details: Photocatalytic degradation with ultraviolet irradiation and titanium dioxide can be an effective treatment for molinate. Studies found that although little molinate degradation is achieved by photolysis alone, in a batch system with 100 to 1000 milligrams per liter of titanium dioxide 50 to 99 percent removal is achieved in less than 30 minutes [1794, 1795]. Photocatalytic degradation of molinate can be effective; however molinate by-products could be formed. One study suggested that the great number of compounds detected during the degradation of molinate shows that the photocatalytic process is complex and that various degradation routes exist [1795]. Cost-effective treatments to complete compound mineralization are usually not practicable, and the presence of by-products during and at the end of the water treatment appears to be unavoidable [1795].
